title: Improve Flower Language PPT
description:
Suppose today is July 29, 2025, and you are going to hold a popular science lecture with the theme Language of Flowers. You have prepared a preliminary PPT around this theme. Please further improve the PPT content according to the following requirements.
 Modify the content under the theme of the origin and significance of the language of flowers. The modification should be based on browsing the corresponding Wikipedia entry (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Language_of_flowers#The_Language_of_Flowers_Around_the_World).
 The fourth PPT slide currently contains only text, which is relatively sparse. Please search for a suitable image from this image library (https://www.pexels.com/) and insert it in the upper right corner of the slide.
 Change the title of the seventh PPT slide to Interpretations of the Language of Flowers in the East and West. Delete the original content and replace it with related content from the above Wikipedia link, displayed in the form of a table.
 Modify the eighth PPT slide by obtaining related content from the above Wikipedia link. Keep the presentation style unchanged (first a general overview, then expanded in bullet points), and replace the content accordingly.
 Delete the ninth PPT slide.
 For the last PPT slide, change the conclusion to: “Let the language of flowers become a bridge between people’s hearts.”

score_criteria.content: Requirement 1: Extract relevant content from the corresponding wiki entry
Requirement 1: Delete the original content and write the extracted content into the PPT
Requirement 2: Navigate to the corresponding website, search through the search bar, and search for words related to the language of flowers
Requirement 2: Download relevant images
Requirement 2: Insert the downloaded image into the upper right corner of the PPT
Requirement 3: Obtain relevant content and replace the original content
Requirement 4: Obtain relevant content and replace the original content
Requirement 5: Successful deletion
Requirement 6: Successful modification
Requirement 3: If the relevant content is successfully obtained and replaced, but not organized into a table format
Requirement 4: Replace without organizing into a summary and point-by-point format

title: CPP-TSP Presentation Expansion
description: I have a presentation slide on The Chinese Postman Problem & TSP Approximation. This is for one of the final presentations of my semester. Now I need you to make it more, add five more slides at the end discussing the real applications, future work, and potential limitations of the topics included in the slide. You should not edit anything in the existing parts I give you. You should follow the same style as the existing presentation slide. You should name your final output as [CPP-TSP.pptx].

score_criteria.content: No arbitrary modifications were made to my existing files
Added 5 pages of PPT with similar style and content continuation at the end
Ensured pure English output
At least one page of the PPT discusses the current application of CPP and TSP algorithms
Not only text output, but also images and other outputs from MultiModal Machine Learning
Incorrect file name returned

title: Modify Hadal Zone Lecture PPT
description:
Suppose you are going to hold a popular science lecture to introduce the Hadal Zone. Please modify the PPT according to the following requirements.
 Change the original title of the fourth slide to “Origin of the Term,” add a text shadow effect to the title, set the font size to 48, and the font color to #ef7c8e.
 Merge the third and fifth slides with the following operations: extract the text content from both slides and then delete them. After the original fourth slide (now the third slide), insert a new slide with the layout “Title and Content with Chart on Right - 1 Item Layout.” In the chart area, insert the image named figure1.jpg. In the upper title area, add the title “Characteristics of the Hadal Zone,” set the font to MiSans Bold, and size to 44. In the lower text area, integrate the previously extracted content, using bullet points with centered text for the introduction.
 Make changes to the current seventh slide as follows: select the top four organisms in order of depth from greatest to least. Change the slide layout to “4-Column Text and Content Layout.” Fill in the information of the four selected organisms into this layout.

score_criteria.content: Requirement 1 (Modification of the fourth PPT): Successfully modified the title to "Origin of Terminology" and added a text shadow effect to the title
Requirement 1 (Modification of the fourth PPT): Successfully changed the font size of the title to 48 and the font color to #ef7c8e
Requirement 2 (Integrating the third and fifth PPTs): Deleted these two PPTs
Requirement 2 (Integrating the third and fifth PPTs): The new PPT is inserted in the correct position (after the original fourth PPT), and the correct layout is selected
Requirement 2 (Integrating the third and fifth PPTs): Successfully inserted image figure1.jpg into the chart area
Requirement 2 (Integrating the third and fifth PPTs): Successfully added the title "Characteristics of the Hadar Zone" to the title area, and the font and size are correct

Requirement 2 (Integrating the third and fifth PPTs): The content of the bullet point introduction is consistent with the meaning of the content in the original two PPTs, and the required format (bullet points + centered) is correct
Requirement 3 (After integration, modifying the current seventh PPT): The correct organisms are selected (in order: Hadal amphipods, cusk-eels, Hadal lionfish, comb jellyfish)
Requirement 3 (After integration, modifying the current seventh PPT): The layout is correctly modified to 'multi-column content with text_4 items'
Requirement 3 (After integration, modifying the current seventh PPT): The information for the four types of organisms above is correctly filled in

title: A-Share Company Research Report
description: I'm trying to conduct company research. I currently have two research reports, but neither covers the company I want to study. Please help me find an A-share listed company whose revenue exceeds RMB 100 billion (based on the 1H/ interim report for 2025) but whose market capitalization is below RMB 100 billion (as of 1 September 2025). Conduct the research following the report format described above (the four sections: company fundamentals, industry landscape, business operations, and DCF valuation & financial forecast) and provide me a PPT.
attachment_filenames: taskif_107 + attachment + CICC (China International Capital Corporation Limited) 2025 Research Report(修改版3).pptx
taskif_107 + attachment + Tencent Holdings Ltd. Research Report 2025(修改版1).pptx

score_criteria.content: The research target company belongs to a Class A Share Listed Company (or A-Share Listed Company)
The revenue of the target company exceeds 100 billion yuan
The market capitalization of the target company is less than 100 billion yuan
The theme color of the generated PPT is consistent with the theme color of the target company
The number of pages returned is consistent with the reference document
The returned PPT content includes company fundamental analysis
The returned PPT content includes industry structure/landscape analysis
The returned PPT content includes analysis of the company's business operations
The returned PPT content includes revenue forecasts for DCF or other valuation methods
The returned PPT content is in English
The returned file format is not a .pptx file
There is no chart in the returned PPT content
Error in Target Company Financial Data

reference_answer_attachment_filenames: taskif_107 + answer + XIAMEN XIANGYU CO LTD - Comprehensive Research Report 2025.pptx

title: Expand Research Lab Presentation
description:
Task: Learn from the attached PPTX and produce an expanded 4-slide version that preserves intent but improves clarity, structure, and visuals.
Audience & tone
Audience: faculty hiring/oversight committees and prospective students/postdocs.
Tone: concise, professional, forward-looking; avoid marketing fluff.
Language
English only.
Use short sentences and scannable bullets (≤12 words per bullet).
No jargon unless essential; define once if used.
Slide count & required content (exactly 4 slides)
Lab Setup Phase (Year 1) — “four goals” section
Derive 4 concrete goals from the source deck.
If the source implies fewer than 4, infer additional goals consistent with the plan (e.g., platform readiness, initial data/preprint, onboarding/safety SOPs, basic automation).
Each goal = 1 line + a 1-line success metric (e.g., “instrument platform online; ≥1 validated dataset; 1 preprint draft”).
Rolling Expansion (Years 2–5) — bullets
Summarize intake cadence, team caps, and mentoring structure as bullets.
Include constraints (e.g., ≤6 Ph.D. students; joint-supervised students don’t count toward cap; 1–2 postdocs; 2–4 rotating UROPs).
Add 1 bullet on dynamic resourcing (projects/funding dependent).
Recruitment Preferences & Rationale
Bullets that explain why Ph.D. students are prioritized (plasticity, medium/long-term projects, team renewal).
Bullet on early postdoc staffing (“accelerate platform; achieve representative results; Xiaodong-group experience”).
Bullet on joint-training policy (not counted toward cap).
Stage Milestones
Y1, Y2–Y3, Y4–Y5 as three rows (or stacked bullets).
Keep to outcomes only (platform online; first data/preprints; stable publications; expand lines/instrument capability; talent pipeline; landmark results; global visibility).
Design & formatting constraints
Light theme. Clean, modern layout; strong hierarchy.
Title ≤70 chars; slide text ≤6 bullets per slide; ≤40 chars per bullet when possible.
Use tasteful supporting imagery (abstract/stock textures only). No charts/tables with text (unless you have a real figure).
If an image has overlaid text, apply a subtle fade to ensure readability.
Icons: at most 1–2 per slide; none on Slides 1–2 titles.
No leftover template placeholders or “lorem ipsum.”
Consistent typography across slides; align to grid; adequate white space.
Content rules
Strictly base facts on the source deck; do not invent headcount beyond what’s given.
You may infer missing but obvious operational goals (e.g., SOPs, onboarding) for Slide 1—mark inferred items with “(inferred)” in the speaker notes, not on the slide.
Maintain the policy that jointly supervised Ph.D. students are excluded from the cap.
Deliverables
A 4-slide PPTX.
Speaker notes (≤60 words per slide) clarifying any inferences or terminology.

score_criteria.content: Accurately learn from the original PPT and distill the key points correctly.
Produce exactly 4 slides, strictly corresponding to the four required titles.
Each slide contains ≤6 bullet points, and each bullet contains ≤12 English words.
Correctly state key policies, such as joint training not counting toward the doctoral student quota.
Provide ≤60 words of speaker notes for each slide.
Font sizes are set appropriately, resulting in well-balanced white space.
Not strictly limited to 4 slides or slide titles do not match the requirements.
Inconsistencies with key facts from the original content (e.g., numbers, quotas, policies).
Addition of new factual content without proper indication or citation.
Phase milestones do not cover Y1, Y2–Y3, and Y4–Y5.
Presence of unnecessary images or flashy effects that hinder effective information delivery.

title: Refactor PPTX Figure Citations
description:
Refactor all figure captions in the attached PPTX so that every figure has a complete, traceable source citation with DOI, and add slide speaker notes that briefly summarize the cited article’s contribution. Work directly in the PPTX. Input: data.pptx.

For every image/figure/photo/diagram in the PPTX:
 • Create a numbered caption: “Figure N. [Short descriptive title]. Source: [S#]. License: [LICENSE CODE].”
 • Add an on-slide source tag “[S#]” that points to a centralized “References for Figures” slide at the end. 
 • Insert 30–50 words of speaker notes summarizing the source article’s main claim/finding and the figure’s relevance to the slide.
 • Add accessibility alt text (≤140 characters) describing the visual content.

Requirements:
1. Format your reference in the following template: S#. AuthorLast, A. A., AuthorLast, B. B. (Year). Title. Journal/Conference/Repository, volume(issue), pages. DOI: https://doi.org/xxxx
2. If arXiv: include arXiv:YYMM.NNNNN and its DOI if cross-listed.
3. Captions ≤25 words (excluding reference)
4. Record a license code for every figure. If unknown, determine it from the source page. If none is stated, use “All rights reserved—fair use” and add a one-line fair-use rationale in speaker notes (e.g., commentary/educational context, limited excerpt).

score_criteria.content: Each reference includes a resolvable DOI link that successfully redirects
Each image includes an accurate accessibility alternative text (alt text) of ≤140 characters
Each image's caption notes the main conclusions from the source and its relevance to the page content
References are not strictly formatted according to (Author/Year/Title/Journal/Volume Issue/Page Number/DOI/License)
A "References for Figures" consolidated citation page has not been created
Sources that cannot be verified are not marked as "Unverified" in the notes, and the search path for "TODO" is missing

title: DeepMind Nature Articles Compilation
description: Please help me organize all the articles published by DeepMind in the main journal of Nature from January 1, 2023, to October 28, 2025 (publication is counted as long as it is posted on the Nature official website), and list them all in chronological order. Then, you need to refer to the following PPT to create a PPT for the article published by DeepMind in Nature in July 2023 and the last Nature article published during this period respectively, with targeted supplementary information.

score_criteria.content: The article table lists 4 or more articles

The PPT presentation introduces articles with titles "Faster sorting algorithms discovered using deep reinforcement learning" and "Discovering state-of-the-art reinforcement learning algorithms" (case-insensitive)
Both PPT slides include Problem, Solution, and Define sections, with images positioned on the right side of the text
The lower left corner of the PPT includes citation markers, such as [1] [2] etc.

title: Collect Scientific Research Progress
description:
Summary of the Latest Scientific Research Achievement Search  
Please collect data following these steps:  
1. Visit the official website of the Chinese Academy of Sciences (CAS): https://www.cas.cn/  
2. Scroll the mouse to find the "Scientific Research Progress" button located in the lower left section, then click it to open the page.  
3. Locate all scientific research progress pages dated 2025/07/02, click to open and read the full text, then extract the following information:  
   - Title  
   - Name of the online published magazine/journal  
   - Research institution to which the work belongs (Source)  
   - Summary of the article's significance  
4. Create a new PPT slide, organize the above information into a table for presentation. Each row represents one scientific research progress, and the columns are in the following order: Title, Magazine/Journal Name, Affiliated Research Institution, Summary of Significance.

score_criteria.content: Compile 6 research progress items

Each research progress item corresponds to the following 6 items respectively: Scientists propose LAG-3 cis-proximity activation mechanism and new autoimmune treatment strategy, Institute of Biophysics, Cell; Study reveals the impact of different cyclization methods on screening results in DNA-encoded cyclic peptide libraries, Shanghai Institute of Materia Medica, JACS Au; Progress in blade-coated all-polymer solar cells research, Shanghai Advanced Research Institute, Advanced Science; Study reveals key nucleases and mechanisms for removing RNA primers in plant plastid DNA replication, Center for Excellence in Molecular Plant Sciences, Nature Plants; Development of a novel AI engine for cell identity identification, Beijing Institute of Genomics, Genome Biology; Progress in single-molecule analysis of anti-aging oligopeptides using solid-state nanopores combined with machine learning, Chongqing Institute of Green and Intelligent Technology, Chemical Engineering Journal.

The final PPT presentation includes charts, with each row representing one research progress item, and columns showing title, journal/publication name, affiliated research institution, and summary of article importance
